Protein names: S-adenosyl-L-methionine-dependent methyltransferase Rv2067c (SAM-dependent MTase Rv2067c) (EC 2.1.1.360) (Methyltransferase Rv2067c)
Domain [CC]: DOMAIN: Consists of the N-terminal S-adenosyl-L-methionine (SAM)-binding catalytic domain (CD) (residues 18-161), the dimerization domain (DD) composed of the large subdomain (LSD) (residues 163-230) and the small subdomain (SSD) (residues 257-291), and the C-terminal domain (CTD) (residues 309-407). {ECO:0000305|PubMed:38129415}.
Catalytic activity: CATALYTIC ACTIVITY: Reaction=L-lysyl(79)-[histone H3] + 3 S-adenosyl-L-methionine = N(6),N(6),N(6)-trimethyl-L-lysyl(79)-[histone H3] + 3 S-adenosyl-L-homocysteine + 3 H(+); Xref=Rhea:RHEA:60328, Rhea:RHEA-COMP:15549, Rhea:RHEA-COMP:15552, ChEBI:CHEBI:15378, ChEBI:CHEBI:29969, ChEBI:CHEBI:57856, ChEBI:CHEBI:59789, ChEBI:CHEBI:61961; EC=2.1.1.360; Evidence={ECO:0000269|PubMed:38129415};
Function [CC]: FUNCTION: Involved in epigenetic reprogramming of the human host cells for the benefit of intracellular survival of the pathogen. Trimethylates 'Lys-79' of human histone H3 forming the so called H3K79me3 mark in the THP-1 macrophages upon M.tuberculosis infection. Acts specifically on free non-nucleosomal histone H3. Alters gene expression of the macrophage post-infection by adding the H3K79me3 mark on genes involved in defense response to increase pathogenesis. Represses methyltransferase DOT1L resulting in reduction of DOT1L-specific nucleosomal H3K79me3 mark and thus in reduced expression of pro-inflammatory cytokines IL6 and TNF leading ultimately to inhibition of caspase-8-dependent apoptosis and enhancement of RIPK3-mediated programmed necrosis. Up-regulates the expression of TMTC1, SESN3 and NLRC3 enabling the pathogen to overcome host inflammatory and oxidative responses. {ECO:0000269|PubMed:38129415}.

Function [CC]: FUNCTION: Histone methyltransferase that specifically trimethylates histone H3 to form H3K79me3. This methylation is required for telomere silencing and for the pachytene checkpoint during the meiotic cell cycle by allowing the recruitment of RAD9 to double strand breaks. Nucleosomes are preferred as substrate compared to free histones. Can bind to DNA (in vitro). {ECO:0000269|PubMed:11029058, ECO:0000269|PubMed:12097318, ECO:0000269|PubMed:12152067, ECO:0000269|PubMed:12574507, ECO:0000269|PubMed:15292170, ECO:0000269|PubMed:15632126, ECO:0000269|PubMed:16166626, ECO:0000269|PubMed:21084562, ECO:0000269|PubMed:9755194}.

Function [CC]: FUNCTION: Histone methyltransferase that trimethylates histone H3 at 'Lys-76' to generate H3K76me3 in ubiquitin-independent manner; prefers H3K76me1 and H3K76me2 over H3K76me0 as substrates (PubMed:16916638, PubMed:25387577, PubMed:38503750). May be involved in cell cycle regulation through the modulation of histone H3 methylation levels (PubMed:16916638). Required for the differentiation of mammal-infective bloodstream forms to insect procyclic forms (PubMed:16916638). Modulates silencing of variant surface glycoproteins (VSGs) and transcriptional switching from one VSG coat to another to maintain monoallelic VSG expression (PubMed:18597556). {ECO:0000269|PubMed:16916638, ECO:0000269|PubMed:18597556, ECO:0000269|PubMed:25387577, ECO:0000269|PubMed:38503750}.

Function [CC]: FUNCTION: Histone methyltransferase that specifically trimethylates histone H3 to form H3K79me3. This methylation is required for telomere silencing and for the pachytene checkpoint during the meiotic cell cycle by allowing the recruitment of RAD9 to double strand breaks. Nucleosomes are preferred as substrate compared to free histone. {ECO:0000250|UniProtKB:Q04089}.
